Issue: LoadImage with ensure_channel_first=True raises ValueError when set_track_meta(False) is active
Decision: pass meta_data explicitly to EnsureChannelFirst when img is not a MetaTensor
Rationale: EnsureChannelFirst.__call__ already accepts a meta_dict parameter; meta_data is always a validated dict at this point in LoadImage.__call__
Author: williams145
Risk: Crash when set_track_meta(False) is used alongside ensure_channel_first=True

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Modified LoadImage.__call__ to conditionally handle channel-first conversion based on whether the loaded image is a MetaTensor. When ensure_channel_first is enabled, the code now checks the image type: if not a MetaTensor, it passes the metadata dictionary to EnsureChannelFirst; if already a MetaTensor, it calls EnsureChannelFirst without the metadata argument. This addresses crashes that occur when set_track_meta(False) is used alongside ensure_channel_first=True.

Justification: The ticket involves modifying the LoadImage class's __call__ method to handle channel-first conversion logic (ensure_channel_first=True), which is directly related to model input processing in the modeling pipeline. Keywords like 'ensure_channel_first', 'LoadImage', and the focus on method modification in a core ML class indicate Modeling Development.
